name: CTA & Purchase Confidence
description: How the primary call-to-action and supporting trust signals should drive confident purchase decisions

heuristics.principle: The primary call to action should be visually distinct and clearly labelled
checkpoints: Add to Cart/Buy button has strong visual prominence
CTA uses contrasting colour that stands out
Button text clearly communicates the action
CTA is sized appropriately for easy interaction

heuristics.principle: The call to action should remain accessible as users scroll, particularly on mobile
checkpoints: Sticky CTA or persistent add-to-cart on mobile
CTA is visible after scrolling past initial viewport
Users can purchase without scrolling back to top
Sticky element does not obscure important content

heuristics.principle: Reassurance such as returns, delivery and guarantees should be visible near the action
checkpoints: Return policy is mentioned near CTA
Delivery information is proximate to purchase button
Security or guarantee badges are visible

heuristics.principle: Users should not encounter preventable blockers at the point of adding to basket
checkpoints: Required selections are clearly indicated before CTA attempt
Error messages are helpful and specific
Users are guided to resolve issues preventing purchase
No unexpected popups or interruptions at CTA
